metadata.author: David Ancor
metadata.stakeholders: Founders; Product Managers; Engineering Teams; Team Admins; Compliance Officers; Site Reliability / DevOps
metadata.parent_okr: Ship c1v v1.0 with metric-traceable specs and live feedback loop
metadata.regulatory_refs: SOC 2 Type II; HIPAA; GDPR; PCI-DSS (scope tbd in Module 4)

audit_summary.notes: Every numeric threshold references an UPPER_SNAKE_CASE constant defined in constants_table.json. No 'and/or' joining two behaviors. No subjective qualifiers (fast, easy, intuitive, seamless). No structural choices (database names, language names, framework names) except as inherited from Module 1 hard constraints.

requirements_table.requirement: The system shall authenticate the Founder's session before accepting a submitted idea prompt.
The system shall persist the submitted idea prompt to the project context with a stable Project ID.
The system shall initiate a conversational intake session scoped to the Project ID.
The system shall present the first intake question to the Founder within FOUNDER_INTAKE_RESPONSE_BUDGET_MS of receiving the idea prompt.
The system shall classify the idea's domain by invoking a configured LLM Provider with the idea prompt and the intake answers.
The system shall loop intake questions until the INTAKE_COMPLETENESS_THRESHOLD is met.
The system shall assemble an initial scope outline containing actors, use cases, and constraints from the intake transcript.
The system shall draft a tech-stack recommendation for each subsystem identified in the scope outline.
The system shall invoke the metric-traceback subroutine for every tech-stack recommendation before the spec is assembled.
The system shall assemble the spec bundle from the scope outline, the traced tech-stack recommendations, and the extracted constants.
The system shall validate the assembled spec against the spec-shape schema and reject the assembly if validation fails.
The system shall complete the spec generation cycle within SPEC_GENERATION_TIMEOUT_SEC from the first Founder answer.
The system shall persist the validated spec to version-controlled storage with status 'draft' and a commit hash.
The system shall record an audit entry for the spec creation with Founder identity, Project ID, commit hash, and timestamp.
The system shall notify the spec's assigned Product Managers that the spec is ready for review.
The system shall return the persisted spec's URL and commit hash to the Founder.
The system shall authenticate the Product Manager's session before exposing the review queue.
The system shall enumerate every spec in 'draft' status assigned to the authenticated Product Manager's review queue.
The system shall return the spec list sorted by submission time ascending and rendered within REVIEW_QUEUE_LOAD_BUDGET_MS.
The system shall verify the Product Manager holds review permission on the selected spec's organization.
The system shall load the spec bundle including the spec document, diagrams, and constants table at the spec's committed hash.
The system shall render the loaded spec bundle in a reviewable view within SPEC_RENDER_BUDGET_MS.
The system shall persist each submitted review comment with Product Manager identity, spec section anchor, and timestamp.
The system shall validate that every 'request_revisions' action includes at least one review comment.
The system shall transition the spec state from 'draft' to the state implied by the review action within STATE_TRANSITION_BUDGET_MS.
The system shall record an audit entry for the state transition with reviewer identity, prior state, new state, comment count, and timestamp.
The system shall notify the spec's Founder of the review decision and attached comments.
The system shall enable the CLI-emission pipeline on approve and block it on reject or revisions_requested.
The system shall notify Engineering Teams subscribed to the project that the spec has transitioned to 'approved' status.
The system shall authenticate the Engineering Team Member's session before accepting a CLI emission request.
The system shall verify the referenced spec's status is exactly 'approved' before proceeding with CLI emission.
The system shall verify the Engineering Team Member holds emit permission on the spec's organization.
The system shall load the approved spec bundle at its committed hash from version-controlled storage.
The system shall generate a SKILL.md export from the spec's agent and capability sections.
The system shall generate a CLAUDE.md export from the spec's conventions and architecture sections.
The system shall generate one MCP tool definition for each interface declared in the spec's interface matrix.
The system shall package the SKILL.md, CLAUDE.md, and MCP tool definitions into a single CLI bundle.
The system shall sign the CLI bundle with the spec's commit hash and the emission timestamp.
The system shall complete CLI bundle generation within CLI_EMISSION_TIMEOUT_SEC of the emission request.
The system shall write the signed CLI bundle to the spec's Version Control repository under the emission path.
The system shall deliver the signed CLI bundle to the Engineering Team Member's registered IDE or CLI client.
The system shall require an IDE or CLI receipt token before marking the emission as delivered.
The system shall record an audit entry for the CLI emission with Engineering Team Member identity, spec commit hash, bundle signature, IDE or CLI client identifier, and timestamp.
The system shall return the bundle location, signature, and commit hash to the Engineering Team Member.
The system shall persist every ingested metric sample to the feedback-loop store with the source observability identifier and a monotonic ingest timestamp.
The system shall bound the overhead of metric ingestion on the customer system to at most MAX_CUSTOMER_SYSTEM_OVERHEAD_PCT of the customer system's measured baseline resources.
The system shall aggregate persisted samples over the AGGREGATION_WINDOW_MIN window on a RECOMMENDATION_CADENCE_MIN schedule.
The system shall compare every aggregate against the spec's declared performance targets for availability, throughput, and latency.
The system shall compute a metric deviation value for every aggregate that falls outside the spec's declared target.
The system shall suppress deviations whose magnitude is below DEVIATION_SUPPRESSION_THRESHOLD to prevent noise surfacing.
The system shall query a configured LLM Provider with each unsuppressed deviation to propose candidate improvements.
The system shall link every candidate improvement to the deviating metric and its source sample identifiers.
The system shall rank candidate improvements by deviation severity and LLM-reported confidence within RECOMMENDATION_LATENCY_SEC of the cycle start.
The system shall cap the delivered recommendation list at MAX_RECOMMENDATIONS_PER_CYCLE entries.
The system shall deliver the ranked recommendation list to the Engineering Team Member's recommendation stream.
The system shall notify Product Managers subscribed to the project of the recommendation cycle completion.
The system shall record an audit entry for the recommendation cycle with the window boundaries, sample count, deviation count, recommendation count, and timestamp.
The system shall persist the Engineering Team Member's action per recommendation with actor identity and timestamp.
The system shall surface a proactive feature candidate when a deviation indicates missing functionality.
The system shall refrain from writing any recommendation back to the customer system in v1 scope.
The system shall receive every draft tech-stack recommendation from the spec generator or recommender with the technology name and the claimed performance capability.
The system shall parse every draft tech-stack recommendation to extract the technology identifier and the claimed performance capability.
The system shall classify the claimed capability into exactly one metric class drawn from availability, throughput, or latency before querying any external source.
The system shall query Documentation Sources for published performance data matching the technology identifier.
The system shall query Cloud Providers for baseline availability and pricing data matching the technology identifier.
The system shall resolve the recommendation to a concrete value for the classified metric using only the cited external sources.
The system shall validate that every recommendation's claimed capability is supported by at least one cited external source.
The system shall compute a traceback confidence score in the range zero to one for every resolved recommendation.
The system shall record the traceback triple for every recommendation with fields recommendation, metric value, metric class, source citation, confidence, and cited_at timestamp.
The system shall flag a recommendation for human review when the traceback confidence score is below TRACEBACK_MIN_CONFIDENCE.
The system shall attach the recorded traceback triple to the recommendation in the spec when the confidence score meets or exceeds TRACEBACK_MIN_CONFIDENCE.
The system shall refuse to emit a spec in which fewer than TRACEBACK_COVERAGE_PCT of tech-stack recommendations carry an attached traceback triple.
The system shall record an audit entry for every traceback event with recommendation identifier, metric class, source citation, confidence, and timestamp.
The system shall return every traced recommendation to the calling use case within TRACEBACK_LATENCY_SEC of receipt.
The system shall authenticate the Engineering Team Member's session before accepting customer connection inputs.
The system shall validate that every supplied endpoint is a well-formed URL resolvable from the c1v egress network before proceeding.
The system shall initiate an OAuth or OIDC authorization handshake with the customer Identity Provider using the supplied configuration.
The system shall reject the authorization when the returned scope contains any write-capability grant.
The system shall persist the Identity Provider credential in encrypted storage with a recorded expiry at ENCRYPTED_CREDENTIAL_EXPIRY_DAYS.
The system shall establish a read-only handle to the customer Version Control endpoint using the credentialed token.
The system shall establish a read-only handle to the customer Observability Tools endpoints using the credentialed token.
The system shall execute a connection-health probe against the Version Control, Observability Tools, and Identity Provider handles and require a success response from each before proceeding.
The system shall measure CPU, memory, and network overhead imposed on the customer system over a baseline probe window of BASELINE_PROBE_WINDOW_SEC.
The system shall reject the connection and surface an overhead violation to the Engineering Team Member when measured overhead exceeds MAX_CUSTOMER_SYSTEM_OVERHEAD_PCT.

The system shall persist the connection record with Version Control handle reference, Observability handle reference, Identity Provider credential reference, measured overhead percentage, and non-invasive status flag.
The system shall schedule a re-authorization event at CONNECTION_REAUTH_DAYS from the connection establishment timestamp.
The system shall record an audit entry for the connection establishment with Engineering Team Member identity, customer organization identifier, endpoint set, measured overhead, and timestamp.
The system shall complete the connection establishment within CONNECTION_ESTABLISHMENT_BUDGET_SEC of receiving the connection inputs.
The system shall return the connection identifier, measured overhead percentage, non-invasive status flag, and re-authorization due date to the Engineering Team Member.
The system shall authenticate every human actor's session via the organization's configured Identity Provider before exposing any protected action.
The system shall record an audit entry for every state-changing action with actor identity, action type, prior state, new state, and timestamp.
The system shall enforce role-based access control on every protected action using the organization's configured role definitions.
The system shall encrypt every persisted credential and secret at rest using the encryption class declared in ENCRYPTION_CLASS.
The system shall retain every audit entry for at least AUDIT_RETENTION_DAYS before archival or deletion.
The system shall bound external-actor-initiated requests to at most RATE_LIMIT_RPM per authenticated actor per minute and reject excess requests with a rate-limit response.
The system shall fall back to the next configured LLM Provider in the organization's fallback ordering when the current provider fails to respond within LLM_PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_SEC.
The system shall emit a structured error response to the initiating actor when a protected action fails, including an error identifier, a retry-eligibility flag, and a support reference identifier.
The system shall emit structured logs and traces for every action at the log level declared in LOG_LEVEL_PROD with trace sampling at TRACE_SAMPLING_RATE.
The system shall export an audit evidence pack in the format declared in EVIDENCE_EXPORT_FORMATS when a compliance export request is received.

delving_questions_applied: What happens on the error path?
What are the concurrency / race conditions?
What are the edge inputs (empty, maximum, malformed)?
What is the timeout if the external actor does not respond?
What gets persisted vs. what stays in memory?
Who is notified on each state change?
What is the cross-cutting implication (auth, audit, RBAC)?
What is the rollback / compensating action?
delving_report.notes: Cross-cutting requirements CC.R01–CC.R10 were added during Phase 9 delve to cover auth, audit, RBAC, encryption, retention, rate limiting, LLM fallback, error shape, observability, and compliance export. These are referenced from individual UC requirements via cross_cutting_ref.
